mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in UNRATE < 4.5% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or failure to replicate in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI inflation and rising Fed funds rate expectations create a 'tightening paradox' that compresses equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-occurring CPI < 0.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining industrial production (INDPRO) signal divergent demand pressures, creating volatility that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in HOUST > 1500 and INDPRO < 104 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals strengthening supply chain capacity, reducing inflationary pressures and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in INDPRO > 105 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 2% growth signals recessionary risks, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 < 2% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in payrolls (PAYEMS) above 200k combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong labor market dynamics, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in PAYEMS > 200k and UNRATE < 4.3% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer finance credit (RSAFS) alongside declining housing starts (HOUST) signals mixed demand signals, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through volatility.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in RSAFS > 720k and HOUST < 1500 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < -0.03.
